data.job_description:
About the Department:

The corporate finance department in the company handles all the financial and investment decisions. It primarily focuses on maximizing shareholder value through long-term and short-term financial planning and the implementation of various strategies. It aims to maximize the value of a business through the planning and execution of resources.
The Role & Responsibilities:

We are seeking a highly skilled finance professional to oversee Record to report, Direct and Indirect taxation, SEZ, Audit processes, financial consolidation, GAAP compliance, and financial analysis. The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring accurate financial reporting, compliance with regulatory standards, and providing key financial insights to support decision-making as per US GAAP, IFRS , IND AS, IGAAP.

Key Role Deliverables:

• Drive monthly, quarterly, and annual closing processes ensuring GAAP compliance.

• Oversee consolidation of financial statements across entities and geographies.

• Manage Direct, Indirect, International, and Withholding tax obligations

• Ensure SEZ compliance and filings for relevant units

• Monitor budgets, cost controls, and financial performance metrics.

• Support capital allocation decisions and investment strategies.

• Implement robust risk assessment frameworks for financial and operational processes.

• Lead ERP upgrades (e.g., D365 F&O) and automation of reconciliation and reporting processes.

• Drive system enhancements for asset capitalization, multi-GAAP reporting, and workflow approvals.

• Govern asset acquisition, capitalization, and impairment processes.

• Ensure accurate depreciation and maintain Fixed Asset Register with SEZ and statutory compliance

Prerequisites:

• Expertise in GAAP, IFRS, and global tax frameworks.

• Strong leadership in ERP implementation and process automation.

• Strategic thinking with hands-on ability to manage audits and reconciliation

• Communication skills, presentation skills

• ERP and dashboard implementation skills

• Consolidation, ESOP, FX, TP, Corporate laws, Disclosure, ESG, lease accounting

• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

• Prior experience in a Big 4 firm or multinational corporation is a plus

• Excellent multi-tasking skills

• Relationship Management with cross functions

• Proactive and entrepreneurial traits; and a willingness to grow and learn on daily basis

Experience
Min 5-12 years of experience (post qualification)
Education

CA, CMA, ACCA
Compensation The compensation structure will be as per industry standards

Department: Finance & Accounts

Reporting to: CFO

Location: Worli, Mumbai, (Near Acharya Atre Metro Station)

About Us

Glide Group operates through three legal entities engaged in online and offline distribution of Beauty, Personal Care, and Wellness categories along with B2B enterprise supplies. We are in a high-growth phase focused on scaling operations, strengthening financial governance, and building data-driven decision systems.

Role Overview

We are looking for a dynamic Finance professional who will act as a business partner to leadership, driving financial governance, commercial control, and performance insights across group entities. This role goes beyond accounting it combines financial controllership, business finance, compliance leadership, and strategic support to enable scalable growth.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Controllership

• Lead end-to-end financial accounting across group companies
• Ensure strong accounting hygiene and timely month-end & year-end closures
• Strengthen internal financial controls and audit readiness
• Oversee vendor accounting, reconciliations, and payment governance
• Manage treasury operations and optimize working capital utilization

Compliance & Governance

• Ensure GST, Income Tax, and ROC compliances across entities
• Lead statutory and tax audits end-to-end
• Maintain compliance calendars and risk monitoring frameworks

Business Finance & Commercial Control

• Implement cost validation and margin monitoring mechanisms
• Identify revenue leakages and drive profitability improvements
• Optimize payable cycles and cash flow efficiency
• Review inventory controls to improve capital efficiency
• Support strategic commercial initiatives and business expansion projects

Management Reporting & Analytics

• Prepare monthly management dashboards and performance review decks
• Analyze key business drivers, profitability, and financial KPIs
• Provide actionable insights to leadership for decision-making
• Collaborate cross-functionally with Operations, Sales, and Supply Chain teams

What We Are Looking For

• Chartered Accountant with 3+ years of experience
• Prior exposure to Retail, Distribution, or Ecommerce businesses preferred
• Strong analytical and commercial mindset
• Proven experience managing teams (6–8 members)
• Ability to work in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment
• High ownership and problem-solving orientation

Why Join Us

• Opportunity to work closely with leadership on strategic decisions
• High visibility role across multiple group entities
• Exposure to fast-growing ecommerce and distribution ecosystem
• Performance-driven growth and leadership opportunities

www.glidebrands.in www.thefaceshop.in www.workstore.in

data.job_title: Chartered Accountant - Finance & Global Accounting Analyst (Pune)
data.job_description:
PayGlocal is a global payments company that fosters global commerce. Our suite of offerings facilitate cross-border payments and solve the challenges surrounding it through three main pillars - the business, the experience and the associated risk.

Founded by finance veterans and experts, our understanding of merchant business, international business, security and regulatory standards help build impactful payment solutions. We partner with large enterprises and export merchants for seamless customer payment journeys.

Be a part of lasting innovation - create new products, new processes and new standards. Join us to help us shape the future of payments.

Position Summary

As a Chartered Accountant – Finance & Global Accounting , you will play a critical role in strengthening the finance backbone of a fast-growing cross-border payments business. You will be responsible for managing multi-entity accounting, supporting cross-border transaction flows, ensuring regulatory compliance across jurisdictions (India, US, UAE), and enabling accurate financial reporting.

This role is ideal for a qualified CA (0–2 years’ experience) who is detail-oriented, analytically strong, and eager to gain exposure to global accounting practices, fintech operations, and scalable financial processes in a high-growth environment.

What will you work on?

- Multi-Entity Accounting: Assist in preparation and finalization of books of accounts across multiple entities and jurisdictions (India, US, UAE, etc.).
- Financial Closures: Support month-end and year-end closing activities, including reconciliations, journal entries, and financial statement preparation.
- Inter-Company Transactions: Manage inter-company accounting, reconciliations, and cross-border charge validations.
- Transfer Pricing Support: Assist in transfer pricing documentation and ensure compliance with inter-company pricing regulations.
- Regulatory & Audit Compliance: Support statutory audits, tax audits, RBI/FEMA/GST compliance, and financial regulatory requirements.
- Cross-Border & FX Accounting: Work on cross-border payment flows, foreign exchange accounting, and revenue reconciliations.
- MIS & Reporting: Prepare MIS reports, variance analysis, financial dashboards, and performance tracking reports for leadership.
- Treasury Coordination: Assist in fund flow tracking, banking coordination, and liquidity monitoring.
- Process Improvement: Contribute to finance automation initiatives, ERP implementation support, and SOP documentation.
- Stakeholder Coordination: Work closely with auditors, consultants, tax advisors, and internal business teams.

Qualifications & Experience

- Qualification: Chartered Accountant (CA) – Qualified.
- Experience: 0–2 years of relevant experience (Articleship experience preferred).
- Accounting Knowledge: Strong understanding of accounting standards, financial statements, and reconciliation processes.
- Analytical Skills: Solid analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail.
- Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in MS Excel; familiarity with ERP/accounting tools such as Zoho, Tally, NetSuite, QuickBooks, etc.
- Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills with ability to coordinate across teams and external stakeholders.
- Work Environment Fit: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced fintech/startup environment with high ownership and accountability.

Additional Information:

• Exposure : Opportunity to work on global accounting practices across India, US, and UAE entities.

• Career Growth : High-ownership role with leadership exposure in a rapidly scaling fintech company.

• Learning & Development : Direct exposure to cross-border payments, regulatory frameworks, and scalable finance processes.

• Benefits : Health insurance and standard company benefits.

Target Profile:

• A recently qualified CA looking to build expertise in global and cross-border accounting.

• Strong interest in fintech, payments, and international finance.

• Detail-oriented professional with strong reconciliation and compliance mindset.

• Comfortable handling multi-entity accounting structures and regulatory complexity.

We are currently seeking a Corporate Finance Specialist to join a growing global finance team, supporting financial reporting, accounting operations, and cross-functional collaboration in a fast-paced, expanding business.

Full-time | Permanent Role

Working Hours: 2:00 PM – 12:00 AM IST (Monday – Friday)

Location: India (Remote)

Benefits: Standard in-country benefits + India holidays

This is a great opportunity for someone who thrives in a dynamic environment, enjoys hands-on accounting work, and wants to be part of an international organization driving innovation in the sporting goods industry.

About the Role In this role, you will be responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, supporting month-end close processes, and ensuring all accounting activities are completed efficiently and in compliance with company standards. You’ll work closely with senior finance leaders and cross-functional teams to support decision-making and business growth.

Key Responsibilities

• Responsible for preparing and maintaining month end close accruals, preparing Journal entries, managing BS and PL - GL reconciliations and reports for management review.
• Entering financial data and transactions into the ledger
• Reviewing financial documents for accuracy and reconciling accounts
• Maintaining and updating the general ledger and chart of accounts
• Creating and analyzing financial statements, such as the balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement
• Identifying and resolving discrepancies in financial entries, reports, and summaries
• Conducting account analysis to ensure that entries and balances are correct
• Collaborating with other departments to complete accurate records and provide accounting support
• Preparing taxes or gathering tax documents
• Supporting the Auditor, Controller and other financial professionals in their duties as needed
• Assisting in the development and implementation of accounting systems, policies, and procedures
• Work with business owners to acquire required approvals and to gain a better understanding of expenses.
• Monitor daily reports related to for triaging Accounts Payable mailboxes and handling of open items through Expense management and Bill Pay tools
• Exercise sound judgment regarding situations that may need supervisor/management involvement.
• Perform other special projects or duties when required.

About You

• Strong communicator and collaborative team player
• Detail-oriented with a proactive and solutions-focused mindset
• Comfortable working in a fast-growing environment with evolving priorities
• Able to exercise sound judgment and escalate issues when necessary

Requirements

Must Haves

• 5+ years’ experience in corporate finance or a similar in-house role
• Chartered Accountant qualification (CA / CPA)
• Strong Excel skills
• Excellent English communication skills (verbal and written)
• Ability to work US / UK-aligned hours

Nice to Have

• Experience with NetSuite
The Team & Culture
You’ll be joining a small but growing finance team, reporting directly to the Financial Controller and working closely with the CFO, operations team, and external auditors. The team values open communication, collaboration, and a willingness to learn and grow together.

Who we are?

Johnson Controls is the global leader for smart, healthy and sustainable buildings. At Johnson Controls, we’ve been making buildings smarter since 1885, and our capabilities, depth of innovation experience, and global reach have been growing ever since. Today, we offer the world’s largest portfolio of building products, technologies, software, and services; we put that portfolio to work to transform the environments where people live, work, learn and play.

This is where Johnson Controls comes in, helping drive the outcomes that matter most. Through a full range of systems and digital solutions, we make your buildings smarter. Their commitment to seamless delivery and customer delight is what enables us to fulfil our brand promise of service excellence. This has been the hallmark of our business for more than a decade.

With deep domain knowledge of the aviation ecosystem, we offer end-to-end ground handling services such as passenger and baggage handling, ramp handling, aircraft interior cleaning, load control and flight operations, and cargo handling services for general, perishable, transshipment, express courier and special cargo.

JOB PURPOSE

We are looking for a dynamic Senior Manager to join our Finance team and to support the Financial Planning and Analysis function by assisting in budgeting, forecasting, financial analysis, and reporting. The role contributes to data-driven decision-making and helps maintain financial discipline across the organization.

Budgeting & Forecasting

• Assist in preparation of annual budgets and rolling forecasts.

At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

Senior – FDI

EY – GDS – Consulting – Finance

As part of our EY Finance Consulting Team, you will play a pivotal role in helping clients leverage finance data intelligence and data lake solutions to drive performance improvement initiatives. This techno-functional role involves assessing finance data processes, analysing data pain points, and designing innovative data-driven solutions. You will work with clients across industries and geographies to unlock insights from finance data and enable smarter decision-making.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Consultant / Senior Consultant with expertise in finance data intelligence, finance data lakes, and finance process – PTP, OTC, RTR & FP&A. Candidates with working knowledge of corporate finance processes, ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, etc.), and finance data analytics are preferred. Experience in FP&A, Financial Accounting, and Reporting domains is an advantage. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to the growth of EY’s finance data intelligence service offering on a global scale.

Your Key Responsibilities

• Expertise on Finance functions including Record to Report (R2R), Procure to Pay (P2P), and Order to Cash (O2C) to understand data flows, account reconciliations, journals, transaction matching, variance analysis, intercompany transactions, and fixed assets accounting.
• Conduct workshops and interviews to perform current state (As-Is) finance data process assessments and design optimized future state processes leveraging data lake architectures and finance data intelligence tools.
• Identify opportunities for process improvement through automation, data integration, technology upgrades, and finance data transformation initiatives.
• Support the implementation and development of finance data lakes, data pipelines, and data governance frameworks.
• Design and implement KPIs, dashboards, and control frameworks for finance data monitoring across O2C, FP&A P2P, and R2R processes.
• Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and translate business requirements into technical data models and solutions.
• Execute data profiling, data quality assessments, and data validation using SQL, Excel, and other analytics tools.

• Manage priorities effectively in a dynamic, high-pressure environment.
• Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required.

Skills And Attributes For Success

• Experience in consulting or finance data intelligence roles is preferred.
• Strong communication skills with the ability to engage confidently with diverse audiences.
• Proficiency in English
• Adaptable and able to work in ambiguous and evolving environments.
• Collaborative team player with the ability to influence and drive consensus.
• Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

To Qualify for the Role, You Must Have

• CA, CFA, CPA, MBA, or Master’s degree in Finance or related field.

Ideally, You’ll Also Have

• Exposure to ERP systems (SAP, Oracle), financial consolidation tools (HFM), and finance data lake platforms.

What We Look For

• A team player with commercial acumen, technical expertise, and eagerness to learn in a fast-paced environment.
• Opportunities to collaborate with EY Advisory teams globally and work with leading businesses across industries.

What Working At EY Offers

At EY, you will work on inspiring and meaningful projects with some of the world’s top companies. We emphasize education, coaching, and practical experience to support your personal and professional growth. You will have the freedom to shape your career with an individual progression plan and take on challenging assignments in an interdisciplinary environment focused on quality and knowledge sharing. Additionally, we offer:

• Support, coaching, and feedback from engaging colleagues.
• Opportunities to develop new skills and advance your career.
• Flexibility to manage your role in a way that suits you.

EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.
